policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate-Related Financial Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released binding guidelines requiring SCBs to integrate climate risk into ICAAP and stress testing by April 2027, with mandatory PD/LGD adjustments for high-emission sectors. Banks must establish board-level climate risk committees and submit quarterly climate risk dashboards starting Q3 2026.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Top 500 Listed Companies with Mandatory Assurance
policy.description: SEBI extended BRSR Core reasonable assurance requirements to top 500 companies by market cap effective FY 2026-27, adding Scope 3 upstream emissions and water stress metrics. Non-compliance will trigger enhanced scrutiny and potential trading restrictions.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Draft Framework for Index-Based and Parametric Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI published draft guidelines enabling insurers to offer parametric covers for flood, drought, and cyclone perils with payouts triggered by pre-defined indices from IMD or approved private data providers. Public comments open until 30 April 2026.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Pillar 3 Climate Risk Disclosure Requirements
policy.description: BCBS issued final Pillar 3 standards requiring G-SIBs to disclose climate-adjusted credit risk metrics including transition-adjusted PDs by January 2028, with phased rollout for other internationally active banks. Physical risk concentration by geography mandated.

policy.title: ISSB Publishes Implementation Guidance for Emerging Market Jurisdictions
policy.description: IFRS Foundation released supplementary guidance addressing proportionality and phased adoption of IFRS S2 for emerging economies, with specific reliefs for Scope 3 data limitations and sector-specific materiality thresholds for agriculture and manufacturing.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Granularity
policy.description: NGFS published updated scenarios incorporating CMIP7 projections with downscaled physical hazard data at 10km resolution, adding compound event modeling for South Asian monsoon variability. Central banks encouraged to adopt for 2027 stress testing cycles.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for India Physical Risk Analytics
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to provide portfolio-level flood and cyclone risk scoring for corporate lending, marking Jupiter's first major Indian banking client. Implementation targets 50,000+ commercial properties by Q4 2026.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $18M Series B for BRSR Compliance Platform Expansion
competitors.description: Bengaluru-based Sprih closed Series B led by Accel and Sequoia India, valuing the company at $85M. Funds earmarked for AI-driven BRSR automation targeting mid-market enterprises and NBFC supply chain ESG scoring.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches Climate-Adjusted PD Model for Emerging Market Banks
competitors.description: Moody's released Climate Credit Risk Score integrating physical and transition risk into corporate PD estimates for India, Brazil, and Southeast Asia. Model claims 15-year forward-looking adjustment capability aligned with RBI stress testing timelines.

competitors.title: First Street Expands Risk Factor Models to India with District-Level Flood Scores
competitors.description: First Street launched India coverage offering flood risk scores for 700+ districts with property-level granularity in metro areas. Partnership with Indian geospatial firms provides ground-truthed elevation data for monsoon flood modeling.

competitors.title: Watershed Partners with Yes Bank for Financed Emissions Measurement
competitors.description: Watershed announced enterprise deal with Yes Bank to measure and report financed emissions across corporate lending portfolio, targeting PCAF-aligned disclosure for FY 2026-27 sustainability report.

competitors.title: XDI Raises $45M Series C, Announces Asset-Level Risk API for Asian Banks
competitors.description: Australian climate risk firm XDI closed Series C led by Temasek, launching real-time API for physical risk scoring targeting APAC banks. India pilot with two undisclosed NBFCs underway for agricultural loan portfolios.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Bank Portfolios: Stress Testing Methodologies and Capital Implications
research.description: RBI staff paper estimates 8-12% increase in credit risk-weighted assets for PSU banks under severe physical risk scenarios by 2035, with agricultural and MSME portfolios most vulnerable. Recommends sector-specific PD overlays for flood-prone districts.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in South Asia: Market Sizing and Regulatory Pathways
research.description: IFC estimates $2.3B annual parametric insurance opportunity in India by 2030, driven by agriculture and SME lending. Report identifies bank-distributed parametric products as highest-growth channel, citing 40% cost advantage over indemnity claims processing.

research.title: sigma 1/2026: Natural Catastrophes in 2025 — Record Losses in Asia
research.description: Swiss Re reports $48B insured losses in Asia-Pacific for 2025, with India accounting for $6.2B from monsoon floods and cyclones. Protection gap estimated at 85% for Indian commercial property, highlighting underinsurance risk for bank collateral.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Sustainable Investment
research.description: World Bank analysis finds 65% of Indian listed companies lack verified Scope 2 emissions data and 78% lack supply chain social metrics. Report calls for capacity building in local data providers and interoperability standards across BRSR, GRI, and ISSB frameworks.

research.title: Climate Risk and Credit Ratings: Incorporating Physical Risk into Sovereign and Corporate Assessments
research.description: CRISIL introduces physical risk adjustment methodology for Indian corporate ratings, finding potential 1-2 notch downgrades for 15% of rated infrastructure and real estate entities in high-exposure coastal and flood-prone regions by 2030.

research.title: Climate Risk Financing for Agriculture: Index Insurance and Credit Linkages
research.description: CPI finds bundling parametric crop insurance with agricultural credit reduces loan default rates by 23% in pilot programs across Maharashtra and Karnataka. Recommends regulatory sandbox for bank-insurer parametric partnerships.

ourRead.headline: RBI's binding climate risk guidelines and IRDAI's parametric framework create a rare 18-month window where StepChange's integrated offering is precisely what Indian banks need.
body: The signals this week point to a single dominant theme: Indian regulators are moving from guidance to enforcement on climate risk, while global competitors are rushing to capture the Indian banking opportunity. The convergence of RBI's final climate risk guidelines, IRDAI's parametric framework draft, and Basel's Pillar 3 finalization creates unprecedented regulatory clarity—and urgency—for StepChange's three strategic bets.

The RBI's March 28 guidelines are the most consequential policy development. Mandatory PD/LGD climate adjustments for high-emission sectors by April 2027 means banks need climate-adjusted credit models now, not in three years. The requirement for quarterly climate risk dashboards starting Q3 2026 gives StepChange less than six months to position its intelligence products as compliance infrastructure. CRISIL's new physical risk rating methodology validates our approach—the question is whether we can deliver sector-specific PD overlays before Moody's captures the market.

Jupiter's HDFC partnership and First Street's India launch are exactly the competitive threats we anticipated, but their timing is instructive. Both are entering with property-level physical risk scores—the 'climate data' layer—without credit model integration. Moody's new Climate Credit Risk Score is more concerning because it bundles hazard data with PD adjustment, targeting the same RBI compliance use case. StepChange's advantage lies in proprietary Indian hazard data (especially monsoon and flood models) and existing relationships with banks who distrust global providers' understanding of Indian credit contexts. XDI's NBFC agricultural pilot signals where the next battleground will be—rural and MSME lending where global players lack ground truth.

The IFC's $2.3B parametric insurance market sizing is the green light StepChange's parametric insurance bet needed. The finding that bank-distributed parametric products offer 40% claims processing savings is our core pitch to lending partners. Combined with CPI's evidence that parametric-credit bundling reduces defaults by 23%, we have a compelling value proposition: climate risk intelligence that simultaneously improves credit quality and enables new insurance revenue. IRDAI's comment period closing April 30 means we should be actively shaping final rules to favor data providers with banking relationships.

One thing to watch: Watershed and Sprih are both winning Indian bank mandates for emissions measurement, which could become an entry point into climate risk analytics. One action: Schedule meetings with RBI-regulated banks before Q2 ends to position StepChange's PD/LGD tools as turnkey solutions for the Q3 2026 dashboard requirement—first-mover advantage on compliance infrastructure is worth more than incremental product features.
